title: RBAC Privilege Vocabulary
description: Canonical contract vocabulary for the request-scoping / privilege rules the backend applies to data-access routes (experiments, datasets, agents, results, observability). Implemented in TraigentBackend request handlers; documented here so SDK / FE / E2E consumers read the contract instead of reverse-engineering it. Advisory documentation: it names the privilege roles and the scoping rules but does NOT constrain any request/response payload.

member.description: Default authenticated principal (JWT user or scoped API key). Sees only resources owned by, or shared with, its own canonical tenant/project context.

admin.description: Platform administrator (admin privilege / admin:all scope). May bypass the owner filter and may target another tenant; see x-scoping-rules.

owner_filter.rule: Data queries inside request handlers are filtered to the requester's own owner/tenant/project context UNLESS the requester is an admin.
owner_filter.description: Non-admin reads and writes are restricted to resources the principal owns or is a project member of. Admins may read across owners.

tenant_scope.rule: The effective tenant is drawn from the canonical (deployment-scoped) request context, never from a user-supplied value, for non-admin principals.
tenant_scope.description: Admins MAY target another tenant's data by supplying X-Tenant-Id; this header is honored ONLY for admin principals. For members it is ignored — tenant is fixed by canonical context (see TraigentBackend tenant-routing policy: request X-Tenant-Id is not trusted for ordinary principals).

project_membership.rule: Access to a project-scoped resource requires the principal to be a member of that project UNLESS the principal is an admin.
project_membership.description: Project membership (see project_membership_schema) gates project-scoped data for members; admins are not membership-gated.

x-admin-bypass-summary: Exactly three controls are relaxed for admins and for no one else: the owner filter, the canonical-tenant pin (admins may override via X-Tenant-Id), and the project-membership requirement. Every relaxation is admin-only and is the security-sensitive surface to audit.

x-references.backend_evidence: TraigentBackend src/routes/experiment_routes.py (owner-filter + admin tenant override + project-membership gates)
